# Accessibility Checklist: Codex marketplace installation path

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-004 requirement quality for accessible Codex install documentation structure, link wording, command context, safety warnings, and install path matrix readability.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist accessibility` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Document Structure

- [x] CHK001 Are heading requirements defined so the Codex install page can be navigated by task section rather than by visual scanning alone? [Spec FR-001; Contract §Required Route Sections]
- [x] CHK002 Are list-based requirements defined for the custom-agent checklist and TOML file inventory so users do not have to parse dense prose? [Spec FR-011; Data Model §Custom-Agent Checklist]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec require headings, lists, tables, and command groups to preserve semantic structure, not just visual grouping? [Resolved: Spec FR-018; Plan §Accessibility Constraints]

## Install Path Matrix

- [x] CHK004 Does the plan require a separate install path matrix for repo-scoped, personal/local, and CLI flows? [Plan §Phase 1]
- [x] CHK005 Does the spec define what accessible alternative or fallback is required if the install path matrix becomes too wide for mobile or difficult for screen-reader navigation? [Resolved: Spec FR-018; Plan §Phase 1]
- [x] CHK006 Does the content contract require table captions, clear headers, or a list/card alternative for the install path matrix when table relationships are not simple? [Resolved: Contract §Required Route Sections]

## Link Accessibility

- [x] CHK007 Do requirements preserve cross-links to DOC-007 and DOC-008 for reference and security/troubleshooting depth? [Spec FR-014]
- [x] CHK008 Do requirements state that link text must describe the destination or purpose without relying on surrounding prose alone? [Resolved: Spec FR-018; Contract §Accessibility Structure Requirements]

## Command Context

- [x] CHK009 Does the manual command-snippet review cover every changed Codex command and path snippet? [Spec FR-017; Quickstart §Command-Snippet Source Review]
- [x] CHK010 Do requirements state that command snippets must be grouped or labeled by platform, install scope, and source of truth so users can distinguish repo, personal/local, and CLI contexts? [Resolved: Spec FR-018; Data Model §Command Snippet]
- [x] CHK011 Does DOC-004 exclude Claude Code install commands except for an explicit cross-link to the DOC-003 path? [Spec FR-015]

## Safety Notices

- [x] CHK012 Does the spec require an install-safety block before users approve writes or network-backed setup? [Spec FR-013; Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK013 Does the spec require safety warnings to be visible in text and not conveyed by color, icon, or callout styling alone? [Resolved: Spec FR-018; Data Model §Safety Notice]

## Notes

- Accessibility gaps were remediated in DOC-004 planning artifacts only. No implementation, runtime, manifest, generated payload, installer behavior, TOML template, hook, release automation, or plugin runtime changes were made.

## Verification Pass

- [x] CHK014 Re-run confirms semantic structure requirements are explicit for headings, lists, tables, and command groups. [Spec FR-018; Data Model §Documentation Entry Point]
- [x] CHK015 Re-run confirms install path matrix accessibility requirements cover clear headers/caption context and a mobile or screen-reader alternative when dense. [Spec FR-018; Contract §Required Route Sections]
- [x] CHK016 Re-run confirms link text requirements describe destination or purpose without relying on surrounding prose. [Spec FR-018; Contract §Accessibility Structure Requirements]
- [x] CHK017 Re-run confirms command snippet requirements are labeled by Codex platform, install scope, and source-of-truth context. [Spec FR-018; Quickstart §Command-Snippet Source Review]
- [x] CHK018 Re-run confirms safety warnings must be visible in text and not color/icon/callout-only. [Spec FR-018; Data Model §Safety Notice]
- [x] CHK019 Re-run confirms accessibility review is part of PR readiness evidence without adding runtime or validation-script scope. [Plan §Validation Plan; Quickstart §Manual Content Review]
